title: ESP Boundary Change Detection via Firewall Configuration Events

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=network (sourcetype=pan:config OR sourcetype=pan:system) earliest=-7d
| where match(object,"(?i)security|zone|interface") OR match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| lookup esp_object_inventory object OUTPUT esp_segment_id
| join type=left max=0 object [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(approved) as approved latest(change_id) as change_id by firewall_object
    | rename firewall_object as object
  ]
| where approved!="true" OR isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, esp_segment_id, change_id, approved
value: We tie ESP security rule, zone, and interface edits to change records so boundary changes to BES Cyber Systems are visible and attributable.
implementation: (1) Forward Panorama or device config logs. (2) Map changed objects to ESP segments in `esp_object_inventory`. (3) Require ITSM linkage for production commits. (4) Weekly compliance dashboard export.
visualization: Timeline (changes), Table (unauthorized edits), Bar chart (by ESP segment)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
